floor_history.headline: Recon: weapon-shift/select UI routine (pad-nibble tests, SE calls, texture upload, 2 sprite emits); plain C, s16 counters drive the sll/sra cadence; full m2c reference

frontier.hypothesis: UNMEASURED (flagship): a full-body draft from tmp/blitz/motion_ShiftControl_m2c.c with S16 loop counters (and s16 cursor/temp locals) reproduces the sll/sra-0x10 cadence and collapses 808 to a residual in the main loop's condition-join scheduling
frontier.mechanism: unwritten body behind replace_with_asmfile; the asm is ordinary compiled C — the only systematic codegen feature is s16-typed induction variables and (s16)(i-base) shift-amount derivation, both direct source-type consequences
frontier.next_probe: structural session: draft into src/text1b.c, `& tools/wteng.ps1 main sandbox motion_ShiftControl --disable all`; get the honest floor, then bisect: pre-pass loop / main loop confirm path / main loop locked path / draw loop / tail

frontier.hypothesis: UNMEASURED: the 10 func_8005C650 call sites must keep their exact per-site arg counts (mixed 3-arg and 4-arg on an unprototyped callee) — the varying counts are what prevents (or shapes) jump2 call-suffix merging of the repeated (id,0x7F,0x7F) calls
frontier.mechanism: cross-jump-call-merge: find_cross_jump merges identical CALL suffixes only when arg-setup insns match; the 4th arg (pan var_a3) present on some sites breaks suffix identity there. Normalizing all to one prototype would let jump2 merge sites the target keeps separate
frontier.next_probe: draft with per-site literal arg lists exactly as m2c shows; if extra/missing merges appear in the sandbox diff around the jals, apply the cross-jump-call-merge levers (arg-count variation) site-by-site

frontier.hypothesis: UNMEASURED: the thrice-repeated special-case condition (D_800A35BC==2 && !(D_800A3568->0x14 & 0x20000) && i==1, with the D_8009BC7C capability-bit else-arm) is a repeated INLINE compound conditional in source (not a helper), and its 3 instances must each be written out to reproduce the block_20/21/23, block_41/94/95 join shapes
frontier.mechanism: GCC 2.7.2 emits short-circuit chains as branch ladders; the m2c goto-joins show partially-shared arms per instance, which only arise from the same source expression re-expanded at each site (a helper call would show jals; a hoisted flag would show one evaluation)
frontier.next_probe: write the condition as a repeated expression; if the branch-sense or join points diverge, sweep operand order per switch-vs-ifchain-branch-sense before restructuring
